D2 Vision L2 Understand Atom

Mapping the Visual Field

trace how far each eye sees in every direction and where its blind spot hides

The monocular field is wide toward the temple, clipped by the nose and brow on the other sides. Perimetry maps this boundary and the blind spot to screen for visual loss.

Move the test stimulus by direction and eccentricity. Inside the gold boundary it is seen; cross the line and it falls outside the field. The shaded patch is the blind spot.
